creator: jbeich@FreeBSD.org
text:
Do you plan to backport comment 4 as well? CVE-2022-21658 affects the standard library which is linked statically (*.rlib) due to unstable ABI. Until something else triggers a rebuild (e.g., OSVERSION bump via freebsd-update) consumers are going to remain vulnerable while users assume a false sense of security.

$ echo 'fn main() {}' foo.rs
$ RUSTC_BOOTSTRAP=1 rustc -Z print-link-args=yes foo.rs | sed 'y/ /\n/' | sed '/libstd/!d'
"/usr/local/lib/rustlib/x86_64-unknown-freebsd/lib/libstd-b94163daca34e02a.rlib"
"/usr/local/lib/rustlib/x86_64-unknown-freebsd/lib/libstd_detect-c0dac963d1e412b5.rlib"
$ tar tf /usr/local/lib/rustlib/x86_64-unknown-freebsd/lib/libstd-b94163daca34e02a.rlib
/
